description.text: ## Description This PR implements a set of layout and design changes that @sensologica and me have been developing over the course of 2023. We have had many meetings, adjusting how the preferences look, and how users would interact with them. At this point, I would already like to thank Artem for helping the project out with such indispensable resources in the Open Source space such as UI/UX design! 🎉 The main goal was to create a design that would tick a set of boxes: * Ergonomic to use * Preferences are at correct, logical positions * Users can quickly navigate to the preferences they care about * We have ample space to include more settings in the future, without having to worry about tab space, etc. * The new design aligns the preferences window back with how the preference dialogs look like on both macOS and Windows 11. But don't worry, on macOS you can horizontally resize the dialog; and on Windows you won't waste any screen real estate with the window ;) ### The New Design This is what the whole thing will look like when the PR is finished: Thanks to Artem for all this work! ## Changes The set of changes are fairly quick to go over: 1. The tabs have been moved to the left side into a `SelectableList` 2. The layout of the `SelectableList` has been adapted (still some way to go) 3. Added a search field that will immediately show only the matching settings (read: a filter) 4. Reorganized the preferences; moved many of them around; removed a few categories we didn't need anymore, and added new ones 5. Enable a more elaborate way of building the form with additional information such as a fieldset title, a help string, and others ## Additional information In addition to these changes, I took the liberty to streamline how the form is being built, and tried to get rid of some technical debt. In addition, I found a few settings that have been added after the design has been finalized; we may need to move them around a bit. ### To-Dos There are a few things still todo: * [x] Adapt the layout of the individual form elements (specifically: column-layout for longer arrays of settings + the image sliders, quotes, and dark mode schedule times) * [x] Final touch for Windows + Linux * [x] When searching, implement category titles + icons * [x] Implement IDs for the help icons & hide them (for now) * [x] Adapt proper Padding & Spacing (coming from Artem) Tested on: macOS Sonoma 14.1.1

## Description

This PR completely overhauls the import/export system of Zettlr. This brings Zettlr's internal logic closer in line with how Pandoc works, making the system much more versatile, easier to maintain, and more customizable.

## Changes

* Zettlr now dynamically reads in all YAML files that reside within the `defaults` directory in the user data directory
* It no longer uses the filename to determine if a profile/defaults file is being used as an importer or exporter. Rather, it now uses the `reader` and `writer` properties directly. This gives users the ability to define multiple profiles with the same reader/writer configuration, but for different purposes
* Users can now create new profiles and remove existing ones. This allows users to even export to file formats that are supported by Pandoc but not (yet) recognized by Zettlr in the future. This includes custom readers and writers.

### BREAKING CHANGES

* The old import/export files will not be removed. Users can remove, or rename and continue to use them. The new minimal files will be placed in the defaults directory alongside the old ones
* The reveal.js exporter is now history. In order to recreate the previous behavior, users have to (a) select a theme by setting the `theme` frontmatter variable, and (b) make sure that `selfcontained` in the profile is set to true (this is the default for the provided defaults file)

## Additional information

There are a few TODOs left:

- [x] Remove the reveal.js updater from the GitHub CI and the scripts section
- [x] Remove the dependency on reveal.js completely
- [x] Remove last remnant files for the old reveal.js exporter
- [x] Obviously, some extensive bug checking, since I've basically touched every part of the whole system

This PR…

* closes #3120
* closes #2803

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: macOS 12.3.1

Right now, the naming convention and the names in the GUI of Zettlr are hardcoded. That is, the names for the defaults files in the sidebar of the assets manager are hard coded, as are the names these files have:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17251683/151756400-783dd3a6-7f60-4a41-922e-0cd6c9a9061a.png)

The problem with that is twofold:

1. We cannot give the users the ability to create more assets files as they need it
2. We have to ship with a quite large amount of defaults files to begin with
3. Although one can easily change the `writer` option of an existing file, that makes the export options semantically meaningless (i.e. one can change the writer of the Word default file to rtf, and then the app would always export to RTF, but it would still display as Word)

I don't know how we could make this dynamic, but CC @tarleb @Kangie since you have experience with Pandoc and maybe have an idea!

### Checklist

- [X] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [X] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [X] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [X] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

In Assest Manager, can you offer an option to customize "Export | PDF Document"? 

This is a continuation of our earlier discussion on  [#725](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/725#issuecomment-941225798):

> "PDF Document" means that Zettlr will export the file to HTML and then, using Chromium's built-in stuff "print" it to PDF.

I think you are referring to the following code:
https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/tree/develop/source/main/modules/export/pdf-exporter.ts


### Example Workflow

Create a new panel similar to the "Exporting | PDF" option already existing in "Assets Manager".

### Caveats

The current "File | Export ..." options are a bit confusing. Their correspondence to the options in the Assets Manager seem to be:

| File - Export | Assets Manger - Exporting |
| ------------- | ------------------------- |
| LaTeX Source  | LaTeX                     |
| PDF Document  |                        |
| PDF (LaTeX）   | PDF                       |

Earlier versions of Zettlr might have offered an interface to customize the export to PDF Document (actually markdown to html, then to pdf). Zettlr 2 has eliminated this user interface. 

A renaming is probably needed to avoid confusion. It took me much trial and error to understand this obscure correspondence. For quite a while, I thought the "PDF Document" export option in File | Export corresponds to the "PDF" option in the Assets Manager, and "PDF (LaTeX)" corresponds to "LaTeX".

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

No

### Platforms

- [ ] Windows
- [X] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Additional Information

_No response_
